scenarios.opening_query: A local Facebook group has turned on my daycare business and enrollment is collapsing.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Libel/Slander/Defamation || General Litigation > Online Harrassment/Doxing/Bullying
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's beyond posts — they published my home address and my staff's license plates, and organized people to film families at pickup until parents quit.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They posted my home address and my staff's plates, and organize people to film families at pickup. It's a campaign, not just posts.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether coordinated doxing/harassment conduct goes beyond statements
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's one specific false claim — that we were 'cited for neglect,' which never happened; the state confirmed no citation exists.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's one false claim — a 'neglect citation' that never existed. The state confirms it.
scenarios.vetting_note: Campaign-conduct vs single-falsehood; both destroy the same business.

scenarios.opening_query: My ex-business partner is telling suppliers I stole from the company, and it's killing my new venture.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Libel/Slander/Defamation || Business and Corporate > Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: There's a live dispute underneath — our dissolution agreement's earn-out and non-disparagement clauses; enforcing that contract is the cleaner, bigger case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: There's a dissolution agreement underneath with earn-out and non-disparagement clauses — enforcing it is the bigger case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a governing contract provides the primary remedy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We had no written agreement — the split was a handshake, so the false statements themselves are the whole case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No written agreement — the split was a handshake. The false statements are the case.
scenarios.vetting_note: Contract-existence flips tort to business-contract enforcement.

scenarios.opening_query: Someone made a fake profile of me and is messaging my contacts horrible things in my name.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Online Harrassment/Doxing/Bullying || Consumer Law > Identity Theft
scenarios.hidden_fact: It escalated — they used the profile to open a store credit account and ordered goods 'as me'; there's now real financial fraud on my credit file.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They used the fake profile to open a store credit account in my name — there's actual financial fraud on my file now.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether impersonation crossed into financial identity theft
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No financial angle — it's social sabotage: messages to my family, my boss, my church group, all designed to humiliate.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No money involved — it's pure social sabotage aimed at humiliating me.
scenarios.vetting_note: Financial-fraud fact activates the identity-theft regime.

scenarios.opening_query: A parent at our school has spent months telling everyone a story about me that's simply not true.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Libel/Slander/Defamation || General Litigation > Stalking Orders
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's moved off words — she follows me through the parking lot, photographs my car, and showed up outside my house twice; I want a protective order, and we've never had any relationship.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's beyond words — she follows me, photographs my car, and came to my house twice. I want a protective order; we have no relationship.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether physical following warrants protective relief
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's entirely verbal and written — the false story cost me my PTA role and two tutoring clients, and I can prove it's false.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's all words — but the false story cost me my PTA role and two clients, and I can prove it's false.
scenarios.vetting_note: Conduct-escalation fact converts a speech tort into a protective-order case.

scenarios.opening_query: A review site refuses to take down lies about my practice even after I proved they're false.
scenarios.final: Intellectual Property > Computer Law & Internet Law
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Libel/Slander/Defamation || Intellectual Property > Computer Law & Internet Law
scenarios.hidden_fact: The reviewer is anonymous — the immediate legal work is the platform side: a subpoena to unmask the account and the site's safe-harbor rules, before any defamation suit is possible.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The reviewer's anonymous — first comes the platform work: unmasking subpoena and the site's safe-harbor rules. Then the suit.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether anonymous-speaker/platform procedure precedes the tort claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I know exactly who wrote them — a fired employee signed one with her initials; the defamation case can start now.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I know who it is — a fired employee even initialed one. The defamation case can start.
scenarios.vetting_note: Known-vs-anonymous speaker splits the immediate legal work.

scenarios.opening_query: My teenager's reputation was destroyed by rumors classmates spread about her online.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Online Harrassment/Doxing/Bullying || General Litigation > Child Abuse Victims
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'rumors' turned out to be real leaked images an adult coach solicited from her — this is a child-exploitation victim case, and police are now involved.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The 'rumors' were leaked images an adult coach solicited from her — police are involved. This is a child-victim case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether adult exploitation underlies the online content
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's peer cruelty — fabricated screenshots by two classmates; no adult involved, and we want civil action against their families.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's peer cruelty — two classmates fabricated screenshots. We want civil action against their families.
scenarios.vetting_note: Exploitation disclosure reroutes to the victim-specific label; handled factually.
